multiplechoiceresponse.label: For some features (e.g., age, thalach), SHAP values are spread widely on both sides of zero in the summary plot. What does this most directly indicate?

choicegroup.choice: The feature has inconsistent effects because the model is unstable.
The feature’s effect on the prediction depends on other features or patient context.
The feature contributes little overall because its effects cancel out.
The feature is less important than features whose SHAP values lie mostly on one side.

div.p: A wide spread of SHAP values on both sides of zero indicates that the same feature can increase or decrease the model’s prediction depending on the specific combination of feature values for a patient. This reflects context-dependent effects and interactions, which are naturally captured by tree-based models. Such variability does not imply instability, irrelevance, or low importance; rather, it highlights why global averages alone are insufficient for understanding model behavior.
